justification: All pass criteria met: 12-month growth projection calculated (~9x, ~446K orders/day); database identified as primary bottleneck (not API servers); 85% disk utilization flagged as critical near-term emergency with 30-60 day timeline; index fix identified as highest ROI first step; phased plan follows indexes → read replicas → storage/partitioning order; sharding explicitly deferred as inappropriate for 4-engineer team; trade-offs include replication lag and cache invalidation complexity.

observations.text: Growth projection math is precise: 50,000 × 1.2^12 ≈ 446K orders/day, ~9x growth clearly stated
Disk emergency called out as critical severity with 'ticking bomb' urgency and immediate action required
Full table scan root cause correctly identified with specific index recommendations (customer_id, delivery composite)
Four-phase plan is well-structured and realistic for team capacity, with Phase 0 emergency actions separated
Sharding and distributed DB explicitly rejected with clear rationale about team size constraints
Trade-offs are thorough: replication lag, cache invalidation, partition cross-query complexity all covered
Pitfalls section directly addresses all failure modes from the rubric
Confidence section is well-calibrated with honest low-confidence areas and stated assumptions
